title: Strata #17 — Red Silent Planes
summary: A vertical abstract painting from the Strata 2026 series, composed around a vivid red field crossed by fine incised white strata. Small pale monolithic forms appear along the surface, creating rhythm, distance and a quiet sense of passage.
concept: In Strata #17 — Red Silent Planes, the red field becomes a silent territory divided by delicate horizontal fault lines. The small monolithic forms seem to rest, emerge or move across these strata, suggesting orientation within an open and undefined landscape. The work speaks of stillness, tension and the subtle passage of time across the surface.
commercial_note: This work belongs to the Strata 2026 series, a body of abstract paintings focused on territory, strata, monoliths and the relationship between silence and structure. Its intense red palette and vertical format give the piece a strong contemporary presence, suitable for collectors, architectural interiors and spaces seeking a bold yet restrained abstract statement.
